article: ChatGPT Go vs Plus vs Pro 2026
content: Two ChatGPT tiers both display "Pro" in your billing portal — one's $100, one's $200, and they're not the same plan. We mapped exactly what separates Go, Plus, and both Pro tiers in 2026. https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/chatgpt-go-vs-plus-vs-pro-2026.html #AITools

article: ChatGPT Go vs Plus vs Pro 2026
content: ChatGPT Go is $8/mo, still shows ads, and runs the same GPT-5.5 Instant base as Plus — so the gap isn't the model, it's the features (no Deep Research, Agent Mode, or GPT-5.5 Pro). For real work, Plus at $20 is the better deal. Where each tier fits: https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/chatgpt-go-vs-plus-vs-pro-2026.html

article: ChatGPT Go vs Plus vs Pro 2026
content: Confused by ChatGPT's plans? Simple rule: most people want Plus ($20). Go ($8) is basically "Free with more room," and Pro only pays off if you hit limits every day. Plain-English breakdown: https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/chatgpt-go-vs-plus-vs-pro-2026.html #AITools

article: ChatGPT Go vs Plus vs Pro 2026
content: Building on @priyanolan's point — Plus vs Pro is really a message-limit decision. Pro $100 = 5x Plus, $200 = 20x, same model suite. If you're not bouncing off caps daily, the extra $80–180 buys you nothing. https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/chatgpt-go-vs-plus-vs-pro-2026.html

article: ChatGPT Go vs Plus vs Pro 2026
content: @marcusveil_ nailed the Go problem. If you're paying $8 just to skip ads… you're not even skipping ads — they're still there. Either stay on Free or go straight to Plus. https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/chatgpt-go-vs-plus-vs-pro-2026.html

article: ChatGPT Go vs Plus vs Pro 2026
content: Weighing just the top tier? We broke down ChatGPT Pro $100 vs $200 on its own — who each is actually built for, and why most people stop at $100. https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/chatgpt-pro-100-vs-200-2026.html

GitHub Copilot did NOT raise its prices on June 1. Pro is still $10. What changed is the meter underneath: chat and agents now burn token-based AI Credits. Completions stay free. Full breakdown of what moved and what didn't: https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/github-copilot-token-billing-2026.html #AITools

The Copilot "10–50x bill" screenshots are real but misleading — they're heavy-agent + expensive-model edge cases, not typical use. The actual change: the fallback to a cheaper model is gone, so agent sessions now hard-stop. What that means for you: https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/github-copilot-token-billing-2026.html

Seeing the Copilot pricing panic? Breathe. Code completions are still free and unlimited. The new meter only touches chat and agents. Before you switch tools, check what you actually use — here's the plain-English version: https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/github-copilot-token-billing-2026.html #AITools

Building on @priyanolan's point — pull your Premium Request Unit history before it ages out. That's your real data. Completion-heavy devs barely feel this; daily-agent users feel it a lot. Don't pick a meter off a screenshot. https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/github-copilot-token-billing-2026.html

@marcusveil_ is right — and the bigger truth: there's no "unlimited" plan left to run to. Even Claude Code is metered. The job now is matching the billing model to how you work, not chasing a deal that no longer exists. https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/github-copilot-token-billing-2026.html

Copilot isn't the outlier — Cursor moved to usage-based billing a year ago and is still fielding complaints. The same pattern, same lessons. Our full Cursor pricing breakdown: https://aitoolgrade.com/blog/cursor-pricing-2026.html
